arts.ttl: Premier League make huge Michael Oliver referee decision amid Arsenal red card controversy
arts.sum: Darren England and Michael Oliver will officiate Premier League games this weekend following controversy during Arsenal's win over Wolves. Opinions have been split on the decision since Lewis-Skelly was sent off by Oliver, while England - who was in the VAR hub in Stockley Park - didn't intervene.

arts.ttl: Report: Manchester United “advancing” on smart transfer deal in major blow for Arsenal
arts.sum: Manchester United are reportedly advancing on a transfer swoop for Arsenal youngster Ayden Heaven. Posting on X, Fabrizio Romano has reported that Man Utd look the clear favourites to snap up the talented 18-year-old, in what could be a big blow for the Gunners. arts.sum: Celtic are in for a busy final week of the winter transfer window leading up to deadline day with the Scottish champions set to bolster Brendan Rodgers' squad for the second half of the season

arts.ttl: Decision close: Arsenal feel they’re in strong position to beat rivals to first-team signing
arts.sum: Arsenal are reportedly waiting for a decision from Norwegian midfield wonderkid Sverre Nypan after strongly stepping up their interest in the player. Nypan looks an exciting prospect but it seems Arsenal are confident they can see off competition for his signature due to promising a role in their first-team straight away, according to the Athletic. arts.ttl: Dusan Vlahovic hands Arsenal transfer boost with new decision after 'significant offer'
arts.sum: Despite rejecting a move to the Saudi Pro League, Vlahovic continues to be linked with a move away from Juventus, with the transfer window set to close on February 3. Vlahovic is not the only striker to be linked with a move to Arsenal before the transfer window closes.

arts.ttl: 'Close': Man United are now on the verge of agreeing fee to sign 'remarkable' Arsenal talent - journalist
Football transfer rumours: Tottenham and Arsenal chase Nico Williams?
arts.sum: This page has got a job to do If part of Tottenham’s problem is that they are tired, then help is on the way. Though the signing of Tyler Dibling looks familiar. The young Saint has been outstanding for Southampton, even amid doom. But now, like Lucas Bergvall, like Archie Gray, he is seen as the solution when he probably ought to be the future. Angel Gomes, of Lille, an England international, will add quality to the midfield and has all the skills to look proper Spurs. A more ambitious target is Athletic Bilbao’s Nico Williams, one of the stars of Euro 2024, also wanted by Arsenal, and available as a buyout – that’s how Athletic do their business – at €58m, which seems cheap. Though does Daniel Levy see it like that? Continue reading...
